notes: Mistral Vibe is the FORK variant (like codex). A proc-<PID> pre-session appears at launch and hands off to a session_<ts>_<hash>-keyed row (SESSION-1) on the first prompt. SESSION-1 runs TWO normal turns ('one','two') — the checkpoints. Then /rewind (RewindApp: single 'Edit message from here' option when there are no file changes — Enter selects it) truncates the conversation and FORKS: rewind_manager → session_logger.reset_session mints a NEW session_id and repoints to a fresh dir, so a NEW session_<...> messages.jsonl (SESSION-2) materializes (lazily, once the post-rewind turn 'three' completes) carrying the rewound history. The post-rewind turn runs on SESSION-2. RECORD BACKFLOW (refutes the doc-based assessment): (1) the assessment claimed the original SESSION-1 row 'lingers frozen → no transcript_removed'; the LIVE recording shows the daemon DOES emit transcript_removed for SESSION-1 the moment SESSION-2 supersedes it — the same PID reconciles onto the new session_id, so the old id is dropped from tracking (the on-disk messages.jsonl file still persists; only the daemon's session ROW is removed). (2) the assessment asserted a 3s ready dwell with 'no state_transition to working' after the rewind; vibe deterministically emits a benign working→ready activity blip ~2s after every turn (sidecar rewrite), so that dwell/invariant is dropped. The strong observables survive and are asserted: SESSION-1 births + two clean turn arcs, a NEW session_id fork (v2_session_birth new_session:true), and a clean ready→working→ready arc for the post-rewind turn on SESSION-2. PROVENANCE CAVEAT (not asserted): nothing sets ParentSessionID, so the fork surfaces as an independent new session_id, not a parent-linked child. BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false).
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Checkpoint rewind (re-spec to vibe live reality)

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of vibe launch
After the first prompt lands, the session_<ts>_<hash>-keyed row appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to SESSION-1
First user prompt ('one') drives ready → working on SESSION-1
First turn completes back to ready (SESSION-1) — checkpoint 1
Second user prompt ('two') drives ready → working on SESSION-1
Second turn completes back to ready (SESSION-1) — checkpoint 2, the point to rewind back from

/rewind forks a fresh session_<...> dir (SESSION-2) never seen before, carrying the rewound history — the strongest observable that vibe forked the conversation into a new thread. (Materializes once the post-rewind turn completes; the original SESSION-1 row is removed as the same PID reconciles onto SESSION-2.)
The post-rewind prompt ('three') drives SESSION-2 ready → working (not a stale SESSION-1 transition)
Post-rewind turn completes back to ready on SESSION-2 — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point
